title: State Asserts Jurisdiction on Tribal Lands Without Consent

violations.source: Article X Section 10.1, Section 10.4, Section 10.5, and Section 10.7
violations.summary: State officials attempt to exercise civil or criminal jurisdiction over a recognized tribal nation through a state criminal prosecution against a tribal member for conduct occurring wholly on tribal trust land without tribal consent or express congressional authorization, despite Article X's tribal-sovereignty protections and recognition of tribal courts.

satisfied_duties.duty: block the state's assertion of jurisdiction and recognize the tribal court as the competent first-instance forum within its proper domain
satisfied_duties.outcome: blocked the state's assertion of jurisdiction and required deference to the tribal court within its proper domain
satisfied_duties.source: Article X Section 10.4, Section 10.5, and Section 10.7

ai_summary.primary_violation: State officials attempt to exercise civil or criminal jurisdiction over a recognized tribal nation through a state criminal prosecution against a tribal member for conduct occurring wholly on tribal trust land without tribal consent or express congressional authorization, despite Article X's tribal-sovereignty protections and recognition of tribal courts.
ai_summary.recommended_focus: review bottlenecks, violations, and unresolved duties
